column4: abnormal entry ignores requirements — 1.6's named scope, stated generally
states the zone mechanics behind the "continue as if unblocked" template
prevention window: a prevention card may be played before there is damage
discard-phase ordering; 5.5 (allies) defensible as a third code
end-of-combat trigger interrupted, but the other consequences still apply
comes back unlocked is not unlocking — states the distinction generally

cumulative / stacking effects: multiple instances of the same effect each apply independently, and each is paid for separately. 1.2 owns wording templates and touches this, but no section owns effect stacking
duration of a prohibition tied to a block attempt, surviving a failed block
cannot play is absolute — it bars playing even for no effect (cycling)
a failed conditional clause does not remove the card's type/keyword
locking to use an ability is not acting, so the recruit-turn restriction does not bite
cumulative / stacking effects: identical burn-blood effects add up rather than overlapping. Same missing topic as R0143; 1.2 is only the nearest neighbour
temporary control meeting minion burning
mandatory vs. optional effects — 1.1's named scope
the "ends unsuccessfully immediately" template and its window-closing effect
a retrieved minion is a new minion: what re-entering play does not remember, incl. NRA history. 1.11 (ash heap retrieval) defensible as a third code
timing relative to the press step, and survival of a premature end of round
block-attempt-impossible template — 3.3 every time
no second block opportunity for Methuselahs who already declined
damage outside combat does not qualify as combat damage

{Derange}'s own restriction on which vampires it may be moved to, extended to the antitribu clan. Fails transfer — no other card's ruling changes; the taxonomy has no clan-identity section and one ruling does not justify one
once the "when successful" window is reached, only same-window effects remain

playing a card that will have no effect: legality of playing a card whose effect cannot apply. Passes both tests (cf. {Dust Up}.3, {Veiled Sight}.1, and the inverse in {Blood Fury}.6) but no section owns it — 1.1 is about reading text, 1.8 about the play/cancel window. 3.2 is the nearest owner here
hand size: what counts against it. NOTE: calibration.md still shows this as a G example, but 6.9 now exists and claims it explicitly — labelled to the current taxonomy
playing a card that will have no effect: a prohibition on the effect does not bar playing the card. Same missing topic as R0533; 4.6 (dodge scope) is the nearest partial owner
contesting, despite hand-size vocabulary — the calibration control
block-attempt-impossible template
abnormal entry: requirements ignored, cost not paid, and the new minion may act
a restriction that persists across the tracked minion leaving and re-entering play; wording is hard to parse but labelled as written
who may block: lifting the who-blocks restriction does not lift the other block requirements
range change takes effect immediately for what may be played
a global stealth penalty also applies to the action to remove the card imposing it
prevent up to X — 4.5's named scope

sect as a trait: precedence of a temporary sect override over a permanent sect change. 5.8 owns sect change via titles and 5.7 sect-derived traits, but nothing owns sect itself — the watch-list gap, cited by name
uncontrolled-region minions are not legal targets
reads the singular in this card's own crypt search. Generalises only to "the singular means one", which fails non-obviousness; and it changes no other card's ruling, so it fails transfer too
block-attempt-impossible template
ordering of a card's benefit against the referendum's own effects
ally acting "as a vampire" — 5.5's named scope
torpid minions still count for a control/possession check
an effect that moves a retainer is not the employ action, so employ-triggered abilities do not fire
draw-then-discard: the discarded card need not be one just drawn
announcement template, plus the not-discarded-on-failure and in-hand-before-replacement clauses
cost to influence vs. cost to recruit for an Imbued
presses apply to the current round only — 4.8's named scope
non-standard hunt actions and what may be targeted
failing to block because of stealth still counts as declining to block
abstain, and the effect reading the final vote disposition at tally

{Marked Path}'s own burn trigger and whether a later action's target set matches the first. Fails transfer — no other card's ruling turns on it; 3.1 owns targets but this is not a rule about targeting
merge is not affected by effects on vampire cards entering play
temporary control ending returns the minion in its current state, torpor included
a Slave entering combat instead of the blocker still satisfies the reaction's conditions
Black Hand is contingent on Sabbat — 5.7's named traits
lock as a cost/effect vs. the Infernal unlock trait; wording reads oddly (locking an already-locked minion) but labelled as written
unlocking to block does not oblige a block attempt or further reactions
announcement template — both codes, in this order, every time
an ally put into play by a card effect still cannot act the turn it arrives
there is a response window before a zero-counter burn resolves
ousting makes minions leave the ready region, which triggers leaves-play effects
retainer and environmental damage are not damage inflicted by the minion
a cost-payment effect restricted to card plays cannot pay for cardless or in-play-card actions
a thwarted diablerie is unsuccessful: no trophy, no blood hunt
requirements do not apply when taking control of a card in play — 6.3's named scope
Edge is granted before the post-bleed pool effect resolves; wording is confusing but labelled as written
duplicate wording of R0247 — labelled identically
one strike card cannot supply the hand strike required by another
block-attempt-impossible template
the trait gained by the action is live immediately after resolution
a Slave entering combat instead of the acting minion cannot use the acting minion's modifier
directed at the controllers of the qualifying cards — 3.1's directed/undirected scope
multiple gain effects from one card remain separate effects; the controller chooses which one is affected
first strike segregates the strikes for the purpose of a strike-affecting effect

minion-phase action ordering: the order in which a Methuselah takes their own actions, and mandatory actions preceding optional ones. 2.4 touches ordering but owns only simultaneous effects — the watch-list gap, cited by name
{Static Virtue}'s own Aye/Orun pairing arithmetic for a newly-readied Laibon. Fails transfer — affects no other card; 5.7 owns Laibon traits but this is this card's retrieval clause
announcement template
a "burn blood to" effect is not a cost: you pay what you can and the effect still fails
costs of canceled cards are still paid
a stolen minion with nowhere to return to is removed from the game
environmental damage — a usable e.g. for 4.4
environmental damage does not count as inflicted by the acting vampire
block-attempt-impossible template
requirements are re-checked: the card's own effect removes its play requirement
the burning card is not a minion, so minion-burn triggers do not fire
the strike may be used even where its payoff cannot apply
the "as played" window applies to out-of-turn masters too
damage still applied when combat ends prematurely
mandatory vs. optional effects
block-attempt-impossible template
wakes are the exception playable in the "as played" window
bleed amount arithmetic on the acting vampire's own bleed
card copy of the G00061 general entry — labelled identically, role E
announcement template
hand size bonuses stack across multiple master phase actions
votes vs. ballots, Priscus subreferendum, and a suppressed title
a cost reduction changes the actual cost for every effect that reads it
a title used to call a referendum is not visible to other cards during it
who chooses the payment currency for a cost
effects queued after combat are lost when a new combat begins
rescue cost reduction applies whoever pays, and is not optional
unlock-phase ordering for a minion returning as the phase begins
sect change does not strip a title — 5.8's named scope
environmental damage — a usable e.g. for 4.4
an ability granting a press is bound to the press step

status conferred by a card when played vs. by an ongoing effect of a card in play, and when other cards may reference that status. Passes transfer (it decides {Open Dossier}, {Dirty Contract}, {Clandestine Contract}) and non-obviousness, but no section owns card-conferred statuses; 1.8 touches the play window and 2.5 persistence
{White Lily}'s own ability requiring her to be the combatant. Fails transfer — reads one clause of one card; 4.1 owns combat participation but no principle is stated
locquipments: a location that is equipment can be paid for by an equipment-cost effect
general rule entry: "immune" means damage is never inflicted, in or out of combat
general rule entry: "as the action is announced" modifiers precede ordinary modifiers
general rule entry: the "as played" window — no replacement, no effects but wakes
general rule entry: what "cancel a card" reaches
general rule entry: special hunt actions take hunt bonuses; blood comes from the target
